openingMessage: Protect the group. Protect the work. Our jobs, our clients, and our reputation depend on it.
leadershipThought: Vague words create confusion. Precision builds trust and leadership. Say what is true, as clearly as possible. Not emotional. Not exaggerated. Not passive aggressive.
opsContent:
Active Jobs (2026-06-21):
• Michael Hutt: Gate out early AM. Starting shift, handover complete. SM Energy Coonskin — 0900 H2S pull at #152 reading 3,000 ppm (carry-forward from 4,000 ppm on 06-18). Flagged tubes low — only 10 left, needs resupply Monday.
• Dennis Easley: Handoff completed @ Gipple 5:30 AM. AOL on Gipple 124H handoff complete with Bryn.
• Justin Pack (H&P 395 rig): Shift change 5:47 AM. Up on pits and rig floor doing rounds. Shakers 0 ppm H2S; suction tank 32 ppm while transferring fluid from frac tanks — monitoring in place. Bit depth ~2,900 ft, fast holing, 0 ppm H2S, rig crew mixing LCM.
• Larry Taylor: SM Energy Gipple 214 — finished pulling packer.
• John Delagarza: AOL at Maralo HL Fed Com #1H (6:41 AM).
• Nathan Contreras: Arrived Shull Hanks 15C 203#, pulling unit still on well. Rerouted to TB Ringo (7:38 AM).
• Caleb Smith: Arrived on location (7:08 AM). Shull Hanks 15C #203H — workover rig in work area, job rescheduled. Rerouted to Bizzell Irvin then Texas Ten (1:12 PM).
• David Hammond: Arrived University 7-20C #3H (7:20 AM). Stalled waiting on flange — moved to next location, planned return after completion.
• Baldemar Bracamontes: Arrived Windham R S 2824WB (7:18 AM).
• Larry Jaramillo: Texas Ten Miles 37C, 6H (7:18 AM) → job complete (9:53 AM) → University 10-1, 10D SWD complete → shop (1:17 PM).
• Louie Zarate: At Emma Jane (poor cell service). SM maintenance foreman Shane and HSE Colton on location — very happy with vessel washouts, location cleanliness after a month on site. Colton called Louie's JSA "very detailed" and told him to keep it up.
• Robert Wright: Job complete, headed to shop (11:29 AM).
• Joseph Popillion: Drogon 2 complete (11:33 AM). Gun Up O 32-29 #4215 complete (1:47 PM), headed to shop.
• Andrew Bailey: Brook N-13WD 204H arrived (10:46 AM) → Brook N-13EQ 217H finished (11:58 AM).
• Justin Fontenot: TB Jeter — starting WAME and line of fire review (10:00 AM).
• Jordan Lemmons: Parks MT complete, released, headed to shop (1:27 PM).
• Dermont Morgan: Finishing location, moved to next job (10:22 AM).
• Brianda Sandoval: Litterbox Battery arrived 1:45 PM, almost done. Back to shop (4:25 PM).
• Stefanie Kirschke: Job done, headed back (2:37 PM).
• Ralph Forbes: Started TRD C 4 (1:29 PM). Having difficulty killing well — ordered 90 more barrels of brine. Back at Carlsbad hotel with trailer (7:54 PM).
• Marylou Guerra: Schlitz A CTB — Travis did one separator, Dawson and Bobby finished separator from prior day, Marylou did NORM. Coming back Monday to continue.
• Dawson Drake: Rig 373 rig-down complete. Added four Elsa hydration bottles on rig 438 — one by drono line, one extra in doghouse, two by sink on mud pit (12:11 PM).
• Marcos Morales: Ovintiv/H&P rig #551 / Fasken Ghost Tree / H2S Supervision — no H2S, done circulating, tripping out of hole (11:28 AM).
• Jacob Hernandez: Shackelford 406H arrived (9:28 AM) → 404H begun (12:36 PM) → 404H complete, atmospheric test confirmed safe, Co Rep released crew, headed to shop (1:51 PM).
• Alex Clubb: Ran safety meeting, placed one under air for first break. Separator known for H2S — 15 ppm initial read. Post-break re-test: zeros on butterfly valve underbelly at AGT. Butterfly valve found damaged internally. Vessel pulled for service. Waiting on Orlando for resolution plan (12:30 PM).
• Anthony Scott: Flagged rain south of Midland by 349 — "Be safe y'all" (6:55 AM).

Carry-Forward:
• [ ] **Alex Clubb — Butterfly valve / H2S separator**: Confirm Orlando's resolution plan before next visit
• [ ] **Michael Hutt — SM Coonskin #152**: 3,000 ppm H2S — confirm status resolved or escalated
• [ ] **Ralph Forbes — TRD C4 well kill**: Confirm well killed successfully; 90 barrels brine ordered
• [ ] **Caleb Smith — Shull Hanks 15C #203H**: Rescheduled — confirm new date and plan
• [ ] **Michael Hutt — Tubes resupply**: Only 10 left, needs order before Monday operations
• [ ] **Marylou Guerra — Schlitz A**: Separator cleaning and NORM work resumes Monday
• [ ] **David Hammond — University 7-20C #3H**: Return after flange issue resolved

fieldNews.headline: Louie Zarate
fieldNews.detail: SM maintenance foreman Shane and HSE Colton visited Emma Jane and were "really happy" with vessel washouts and location cleanliness after a month on site. Colton specifically called out Louie's JSA as "very detailed" and told him to keep it up.

fieldNews.headline: Jacob Hernandez
fieldNews.detail: Shackelford 404H complete with atmospheric test conducted and area confirmed safe before Co Rep released crew.

fieldNews.headline: Dawson Drake
fieldNews.detail: Proactively added four Elsa hydration bottles across rig 438 ahead of summer heat.

fieldNews.headline: Justin Pack
fieldNews.detail: Up on pits and rig floor doing monitoring rounds immediately at shift change; flagged suction tank H2S reading proactively.

todaysChallenge: Before you speak on the next job — ask yourself: What outcome am I trying to create?
todaysChallengeSubtext: If you can't answer that, don't speak yet. Outcome-driven communication solves problems. Emotion-driven communication creates them.
